title: Niphobolus pertusus
taxonomy: Niphobolus pertusus is a species of plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Niphobolus. The genus Niphobolus is part of the larger group of plants known as polypodiophytes. Polypodiophytes are a group of vascular plants that belong to the division Pteridophyta. They are often referred to as ferns, and are characterized by the production of spores rather than seeds.
uses: Niphobolus pertusus, commonly known as Maidenhair spleenwort, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing pain and inflammation. The plant is also used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some cultures, it is used to treat respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Niphobolus pertusus for medicinal purposes.

intro: Niphobolus pertusus, also known as the Indian pennywort, is a plant species in the family Polypodiaceae, characterized by its distinctive round leaves that resemble coins. Traditionally, Indian pennywort has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits such as reducing stress, improving cognitive function, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Indian pennywort include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Indian pennywort is commonly prepared as a tea or used in herbal supplements, as well as being made into a paste for topical application, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
